mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in nonfarm payrolls signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:payrolls growth positively correlates with consumption
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of months when PAYEMS grows month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 6% forces the Federal Reserve into aggressive tightening, raising real discount rates and crushing equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 6%.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of high CPI inflation and a near-zero nominal Fed funds rate produces deeply negative real policy rates that initially boost equities, but as markets price abrupt Fed catch-up the subsequent repricing of forward rates causes negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of historical joint-signal episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly real GDP growth predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the economy is in a late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets; this relationship is null or reverses in early-cycle recoveries.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE is below 4.5%, negative GDPC1 growth fails to predict negative 60-day returns (positive returns in > 50% of instances); or the relationship does not weaken in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes demand collapse and negative earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining more than 5% month-over-month reflect rising mortgage rates and weakening construction activity, leading to negative wealth effects and lower cyclical exposure in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months when HOUST declines exceed 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the federal funds rate signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the cost of capital and reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows, generating negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production contracts simultaneously with rising unemployment, the joint signal confirms a broad supply-demand recessionary dynamic that produces deeply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0 and UNRATE month-over-month change > 0
failure_condition: When both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rise in the same month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of joint-signal instances.
